description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone block with a rough, weathered surface and a predominantly brownish-tan coloration. One face of the block features a series of diagonal incised lines on the left and a pattern of small, deep triangular or wedge-shaped carvings on the right, suggesting it may have served a decorative or communicative purpose. The opposite face is unmarked and displays natural stone texture with some discoloration and surface pitting. The block is relatively thin compared to its width and height, with uneven, jagged edges and a generally flat profile. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it could be a fragment of a larger architectural or boundary element, possibly from an ancient or historical urban setting.
description: A roughly triangular, weathered stone block with incised lines and wedge-shaped carvings on one face, and a natural, unmarked stone surface on the reverse.
description_view_0: The front face shows diagonal incised lines on the left and a grid of wedge-shaped carvings on the right, with a rough, tan stone texture.
description_view_1: A thin edge view reveals the block's narrow profile and uneven thickness, with visible roughness along the side.
description_view_2: The back face is unmarked, displaying a natural, mottled stone surface with some discoloration and pitting.
description_view_3: Another thin edge view, similar to view 1, showing the irregular, jagged edge and the stone's layered structure.
attribute_location_context: ancient urban boundary or decorative wall fragment
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with pitting, discoloration, and chipped edges, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible incised lines and wedge-shaped carvings on one face, possibly decorative or symbolic.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous wall or boundary when combined with similar blocks.
